questions.question: For an undirected graph with 5 vertices and 4 edges, which representation would be more space-efficient and why? (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Calculate max possible edges: For V=5, max edges = V(V-1)/2 = 10.
Current edges E=4, which is less than half of 10, making it a sparse graph.
An adjacency list uses O(V+E)=O(5+4)=O(9) space, while a matrix uses O(V²)=O(25) space. Therefore, the list is more space-efficient.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the adjacency matrix and adjacency list data structures. Your answer should cover space complexity, time complexity for key operations, and suitability for different types of graphs. (8 marks)
expectedPoints: Space Complexity: Matrix is O(V²); List is O(V+E).
Edge Lookup: Matrix is O(1); List is O(deg(u)) which can be O(V) in worst case.
Finding Neighbors: Matrix is O(V); List is O(deg(u)) which is optimal.
Adding an Edge: Both are efficient (O(1) for matrix, typically O(1) for list).
Suitability: Matrix is best for dense graphs and frequent edge queries. List is best for sparse graphs and traversal-heavy algorithms.
Additional point: Discussion of symmetry in matrix for undirected graphs.
Additional point: How weights are handled in each representation.
